retag_note: Retagged 2026-05-04 from MECH-303 to MECH-304. Single-unit IL encoding of conditioned inhibitors (cue-specific, teaching-signal-driven) belongs to the MECH-304 system, not the contextual passive safety system (MECH-303).

source.title: Encoding of conditioned inhibitors of fear in the infralimbic cortex

confidence_rationale: Single-unit recordings show IL neurons encode conditioned inhibitors specifically -- distinct from reward-cue encoding. Three subpopulations: (1) neurons responding exclusively to the fear+safety compound, (2) neurons responding to both fear+safety and reward cues, (3) bidirectional neurons (excited by fear+safety, inhibited by fear cue). Negative correlation between IL excitation and freezing is the functional linkage. This is direct electrophysiological evidence that the IL node participates in encoding the MECH-304 cue-specific conditioned inhibition system, and that this encoding is dissociable from reward. The partial overlap with reward neurons qualifies but does not refute MECH-304's parallel-but-distinct encoding claim.
failure_signatures: A subpopulation of IL neurons responded to both the fear+safety cue AND the reward cue, suggesting partial overlap between the cue-specific safety encoding node and the reward store -- this is a direct qualifier on MECH-304's 'parallel-but-distinct' claim.

Neural activity was measured in response to the compound fear+safety cue, not to the safety cue alone. Most neurons responded to the compound but not to safety-alone, so the predictive encoding during safety-only contexts (the MECH-304 cue-specificity target) is not directly measured.
Only IL was recorded; no simultaneous recording from dorsal striatum or dlPFC that would confirm the full cortico-striatal encoding substrate that MECH-304 posits.

mapping.source_claim_statement: IL neurons show excitatory responses specifically to conditioned inhibitors (fear+safety compound cue), with activity negatively correlated with fear expression. A subset also responds to reward cues. Neural encoding is safety-cue-specific and distinct from pure fear or pure reward responses.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-304 posits a cortico-striatal encoding substrate (dorsal striatum + dlPFC) with an IL->CeA expression pathway for cue-specific conditioned safety. The IL single-unit data establish that IL neurons encode conditioned inhibitors as active representations -- not merely a passive absence of fear-cue responding -- and that this encoding is functionally dissociable from reward-cue encoding. The partial overlap with reward-responding neurons is a direct qualifier: MECH-304 asserts the cue-specific safety store is parallel-but-distinct from the reward store, which this data qualifies (some neurons are shared). This paper covers the IL encoding node, complementing the Ng & Sangha 2023 Neuropsychopharmacology paper on the IL->CeA expression projection.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The compound-cue paradigm conflates the predictive encoding of safety with the conflict-resolution computation (safety overriding fear). MECH-304 is primarily a claim about the cue-specific predictive safety store and its expression pathway (IL->CeA), not necessarily about the conflict-resolution node itself.
mapping.source_context: Male Long Evans rats, single-unit recordings in IL, Pavlovian conditional discrimination task with fear, reward, and safety cues.

confidence_components.notes: High quality electrophysiology from a well-characterised paradigm. Mapping fidelity is good but docked because the compound-cue context differs from the standalone safety-cue prediction scenario MECH-304 targets. The partial overlap with reward neurons is a genuine qualifier on the 'distinct' claim, recorded directly in the data.
